summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that ProcessingInstruction and Comment data containing serialization terminators (?> and -->) are emitted verbatim by XMLSerializer, breaking node boundaries and allowing injection of arbitrary XML elements that are parsed as real document content upon re-parsing. The CDATA fix (GHSA-wh4c-j3r5-mjhp) did not extend the same splitting/validation protection to PI or Comment nodes.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate three injection scenarios: (1) PI data containing ?> breaks the PI boundary and injects an additional ProcessingInstruction into the re-parsed document, (2) Comment data containing --> breaks the comment boundary and injects an arbitrary XML element into the re-parsed document, and (3) PI injection succeeds even when requireWellFormed=true because the serializer performs no PI data validation.
actual_observed_result: All three tests passed: PI mutation injected an attacker-controlled ProcessingInstruction confirmed by the proof string 'EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED' in the re-parsed document. Comment mutation injected an attacker-controlled element confirmed by the tag name 'EVOHUNT_COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED' in the re-parsed document. PI injection also succeeded with requireWellFormed=true, confirmed by 'EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED_RWF'.
verification_method: The exploit script creates PI and Comment nodes with data containing serialization terminators, serializes the document, re-parses the serialized output, and searches for proof strings and injected element names in the re-parsed DOM. This distinguishes real injection from generic parsing errors or unrelated crashes.
evidence_produced_by_run: Test 1: Serialized PI output '...href="style.css"?><?inject EVOHUNT_PI_INJECTION_CONFIRMED ?>?>...' shows broken PI boundary with injected PI.
Test 2: Serialized Comment output '...<!--original--><EVOHUNT_COMMENT_INJECTION_CONFIRMED/><!--restored-->...' shows broken comment boundary with injected element.
Test 3: Serialized PI output with requireWellFormed=true shows same boundary breakage, confirming no PI data validation exists in the serializer.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the 0.9.9 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
